title: Treehouse Of Horror Ii

jokes.text: Before last year's Halloween show... I warned you not to let your children watch... but you did anyway. Well, this year's episode is even worse.
jokes.setup: Parental warning about inappropriate content for children
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging parents ignored the warning and doubling down with an even worse episode
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta humor that breaks the fourth wall effectively, acknowledging the show's own transgressive nature

jokes.text: Well, if you didn't listen to me last time... you're not going to now. Enjoy the show.
jokes.setup: Continued parental warning
jokes.punchline: Resigned acceptance that the warning is pointless
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of parental defeat, economical and relatable

jokes.text: remember, the presidential primaries... are only a few months away. Hey, if you don't like it, go to Russia.
jokes.setup: Halloween newscast ending on a scary note
jokes.punchline: Presidential primaries being scarier than Halloween, followed by dated Cold War dismissal
jokes.explanation: Decent political satire but feels somewhat generic and dated

jokes.text: Aren't you a little old for this? You're not even wearing costumes. Hand over the candy, old dude, or we egg your house.
jokes.setup: Teenagers trick-or-treating without costumes
jokes.punchline: Escalating from no costumes to extortion threat
jokes.explanation: Captures teenage delinquent behavior well but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Here you go, kids. Heh-heh... heh. Lousy punks. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Ned giving in to the teenagers' demands
jokes.punchline: Muttering insults under his breath, then Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ned, plus Homer's iconic catchphrase

jokes.text: I didn't select this costume for mobility. I wore it to salute the noble Native Americans of the Pacific Northwest.
jokes.setup: Lisa walking slowly in her costume
jokes.punchline: Overly earnest explanation of costume choice prioritizing cultural respect over practicality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - overly serious about cultural sensitivity even when inconvenient

jokes.text: Yaahh! Ha-ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably scaring the kids
jokes.punchline: His gleeful reaction to frightening children
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy, fits Homer's childish nature but unremarkable

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, everybody in this family... will have a bad nightmare tonight. Oh, yeah, three bad nightmares. I'd like to see that. Heh-heh-heh.
jokes.setup: Marge warning about nightmares from too much candy
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking the fourth wall by referencing the three-story structure of the episode
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that sets up the episode structure while staying in character

jokes.text: What a dump! Why would Princess Grace live here?
jokes.setup: Homer looking at what appears to be a shabby location
jokes.punchline: Confusing Monaco (the country) with what he's seeing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's ignorance about geography and celebrity culture

jokes.text: Dad, that's Monaco. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's confusion about Princess Grace
jokes.punchline: Lisa's correction followed by Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Lisa as straight man and Homer's iconic catchphrase

jokes.text: I can do that, but I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Presumably someone asking Homer to do something
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy honesty about not wanting to make effort
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Homer line that captures his lazy honesty

jokes.text: It is a monkey's paw, dating back to Al-Al Ben Abdallah.
jokes.setup: Introducing the mystical monkey's paw
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake-sounding Arabic name with stuttering effect
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but relies somewhat on ethnic humor stereotypes

jokes.text: I was once president of Algeria. I don't want to hear your life story. Paw me.
jokes.setup: Shopkeeper explaining the paw's curse with personal example
jokes.punchline: Homer's impatient dismissal and crude demand
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's impatience and crude directness

jokes.text: Why, at that little shop right over... there. Oh, it was over there.
jokes.setup: Marge asking where Homer got the monkey's paw
jokes.punchline: The mysterious shop has already disappeared
jokes.explanation: Classic horror trope executed competently but predictably

jokes.text: You'll be sorry. Flight 7, Marrakesh to Springfield, now boarding.
jokes.setup: Shopkeeper's ominous warning
jokes.punchline: Mundane flight announcement immediately after, suggesting he's just leaving normally
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the mysterious shopkeeper trope with mundane reality

jokes.text: Don't shoot! Don't shoot! They're souvenirs. You must pay a fine of two American dollars. Okay.
jokes.setup: Dramatic airport security confrontation
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic small fine and Homer's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax humor deflating the tension with bureaucratic mundanity

jokes.text: Oh, no! Maggie made a wish.
jokes.setup: Family arguing over who gets to make the first wish
jokes.punchline: Baby unexpectedly beats them all to it
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection using the baby as an unexpected agent

jokes.text: Oh, my land! Ooh.! A luxury car. Good baby. Good Maggie. Ohh, a new pacifier. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Maggie's wish appearing to grant them a luxury car
jokes.punchline: Revealing she actually just wished for a pacifier
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that perfectly captures a baby's priorities vs. adult assumptions

jokes.text: Look, everybody. My purse exploded!
jokes.setup: Family suddenly becoming rich and famous
jokes.punchline: Marge's purse literally bursting with money
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows rather than tells their sudden wealth

jokes.text: I'm terrible sorry. I have absolutely nothing untilJune. Ah, the Simpsons! Right this way, please.
jokes.setup: Restaurant being fully booked
jokes.punchline: Immediately changing tune when recognizing celebrities
jokes.explanation: Classic celebrity treatment satire, well-executed if predictable

jokes.text: If I hear one more thing about the Simpsons... I swear I'll scream. At first they were cute, but now they're annoying. 18 bucks for this? What a rip-off.
jokes.setup: The Simpsons' overwhelming fame and ubiquity
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on the show's own potential for overexposure and merchandising
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware commentary on the show's own cultural saturation and merchandising

jokes.text: Is there anything they won't do?
jokes.setup: Continued commentary on the Simpsons' ubiquity
jokes.punchline: Meta question about the show's willingness to do anything for attention
jokes.explanation: Continues the meta-commentary theme effectively

jokes.text: Sorry about the Falklands. Oh, forget it. We knew they were yours.
jokes.setup: World peace being achieved
jokes.punchline: Even contentious international disputes are resolved amicably
jokes.explanation: Smart political reference showing the absurdity of perfect world peace

jokes.text: Well, won't be needing these anymore.
jokes.setup: World peace being achieved
jokes.punchline: Military casually discarding weapons
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual showing the logical consequence of world peace

jokes.text: Foolish humans. Oh, yes, Kodos. Earth is now ripe for the plucking.
jokes.setup: Humans achieving world peace
jokes.punchline: Aliens seeing this as the perfect opportunity to invade
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic twist showing unintended consequences of the 'perfect' wish

jokes.text: Your superior intellect is no match for our puny weapons. Gordon, do something. What do you want me to do? I'm a baker now.
jokes.setup: Humans trying to fight technologically superior aliens
jokes.punchline: Military leader now being a baker due to world peace disarmament
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the world peace consequences with perfect character detail

jokes.text: Nice hot mustard, good bread... turkey's a little dry-- The turkey's a little dry?! Oh, foul the cursed thing! What demon from the depths of hell created thee?
jokes.setup: Homer making a careful wish to avoid monkey's paw consequences
jokes.punchline: Still finding fault with the sandwich and dramatically overreacting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Homer's inability to be satisfied even when getting exactly what he asked for. The dramatic escalation from mild complaint to Shakespearean curse is brilliant.

jokes.text: Hey, is that one of those monkey paw dealies... that lets you wish for things? Yeah, but I got to warn you that this thing is-- Yeah, that's what it is. You want to try it?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to warn Ned about the monkey's paw
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately abandoning the warning when Ned shows interest
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's selfishness and inability to follow through on good intentions

jokes.text: Aahh! He's got a board with a nail in it! Enslave humanity, will ya?! Run, Kodos!
jokes.setup: Technologically advanced aliens conquering Earth
jokes.punchline: Being defeated by the most primitive weapon imaginable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy inverting the typical alien invasion trope with the most primitive possible solution

jokes.text: Okely-dokely.
jokes.setup: Homer offering the monkey's paw to Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic cheerful acceptance
jokes.explanation: Pure Ned Flanders - his catchphrase perfectly captures his naive optimism

jokes.text: That board with the nail in it may have defeated us... but the humans won't stop there. They'll make bigger boards and bigger nails. Soon they will make a board with a nail so big... it will destroy them all!
jokes.setup: Aliens being defeated by primitive weapon
jokes.punchline: Predicting humanity's self-destruction through arms race with increasingly large boards and nails
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation of the board-with-nail gag into brilliant commentary on arms races and human self-destruction. The logical absurdity is perfect.

jokes.text: I wish I had a monkey's paw.
jokes.setup: Ned using the monkey's paw after saving the world
jokes.punchline: Creating infinite loop of monkey's paws
jokes.explanation: Smart recursive joke that creates logical absurdity while maintaining story structure

jokes.text: Presented for your consideration:: Springfield-- an average little town... with a not-so-average monster.
jokes.setup: Rod Serling Twilight Zone parody opening
jokes.punchline: Classic Twilight Zone setup being applied to Springfield
jokes.explanation: Good parody setup that establishes the Twilight Zone homage effectively

jokes.text: And did I mention that the monster... is a ten-year-old boy? Quite a twist, huh? Betcha didn't see that one coming.
jokes.setup: Building up the mysterious monster
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to acknowledge the 'twist' while making it obvious
jokes.explanation: Self-aware parody that acknowledges its own obviousness while maintaining the Twilight Zone pastiche

jokes.text: Every day, same old cat. I'll make him more interesting. Much better. Oh, good, the curtains are on fire.
jokes.setup: Bart being bored with the family cat
jokes.punchline: Transforming it into something monstrous, then casually noting the fire
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's casual attitude toward chaos and destruction

jokes.text: Much better. Oh, good, the curtains are on fire.
jokes.setup: Bart has just created chaos
jokes.punchline: Casual approval of additional destruction
jokes.explanation: Bart's psychopathic cheerfulness about destruction is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's good that you made that awful thing, Bart. It's real good.
jokes.setup: Bart creating something horrifying
jokes.punchline: Marge's forced positive response due to fear
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's maternal instincts conflicting with terror

jokes.text: He gets it from your side of the family, you know. No monsters on my side.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bart's supernatural powers
jokes.punchline: Typical marital blame-shifting about family genetics
jokes.explanation: Relatable marital humor that grounds the supernatural elements in domestic reality

jokes.text: Oh, wait. You're the little dude with the gnarly powers.
jokes.setup: Otto initially refusing to let Bart drive
jokes.punchline: Casual recognition and acceptance of Bart's supernatural abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment - laid-back acceptance of the impossible

jokes.text: America was now discovered in 1942 by... 'some guy.' And our country isn't called America anymore. It's Bonerland.
jokes.setup: History being changed to match Bart's test answers
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous historical 'facts' and juvenile country name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the absurd consequences of Bart's power, with perfectly juvenile humor

jokes.text: I'm a stupid moron with an ugly face and a big butt... and my butt smells and I like to kiss my own butt.
jokes.setup: Bart's prank call to Moe's Tavern
jokes.punchline: Moe unwittingly reading increasingly ridiculous self-insults
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart prank escalated to absurd levels, though relies on juvenile humor

jokes.text: The ball is turning into a fat, bald guy.
jokes.setup: Homer watching football, hoping to win a bet
jokes.punchline: Bart transforming the football into Homer mid-kick
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual absurdity with perfect deadpan delivery from the announcer

jokes.text: It's no good, and you know what we say... Every time something strange happens-- it's good that Bart did that. It's very good.
jokes.setup: Homer losing his bet due to Bart's interference
jokes.punchline: Forced positive reaction despite clear frustration
jokes.explanation: Good running gag showing the forced positivity everyone must maintain

jokes.text: 346 consecutive hours... and all because of one little boy who-- who won't let me stop!
jokes.setup: Krusty's show running continuously
jokes.punchline: His exhausted desperation while being forced to continue
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the horrific consequences of Bart's power for his victims

jokes.text: Then, when he's least expecting it, bash his head in. End of monster. Aaaaaaaaaahhh!
jokes.setup: Homer plotting against Bart while thinking he can't hear thoughts
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately hearing and reacting to the murderous plan
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff showing Homer's incompetence and the futility of fighting Bart

jokes.text: I'm not nodding. It's the air conditioning.
jokes.setup: Dr. Marvin commenting on Homer agreeing with his theory
jokes.punchline: Homer's head has been transformed into a jack-in-the-box that keeps popping up
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag with Homer trying to maintain dignity despite his absurd transformation

jokes.text: I'm not nodding. It's the air conditioning.
jokes.setup: Therapist noting Homer agreeing with diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd excuse for involuntary movement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer denial with ridiculous excuse

jokes.text: You got it. Oh! Hey, hey. Oh, oh. Thanks, boy. I love you, Dad. I love you, son.
jokes.setup: Bart giving Homer his body back
jokes.punchline: Genuine father-son bonding moment
jokes.explanation: Heartwarming moment that provides emotional payoff to the psychological horror premise

jokes.text: Little do they realize... their days of suckling at my teat are numbered.
jokes.setup: Burns observing his workers
jokes.punchline: Grotesque maternal metaphor for employment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line combining his aristocratic vocabulary with disturbing imagery

jokes.text: Very well. Eeny, meeny, miny-- Blugh! Him!
jokes.setup: Burns deciding to randomly fire someone
jokes.punchline: Using childish selection method then having visceral reaction to choosing Homer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' arbitrary cruelty and particular disdain for Homer

jokes.text: The greatest breakthrough in labor relations... since the cat-o'-nine-tails!
jokes.setup: Burns presenting his robot worker innovation
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a torture device used for punishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns line revealing his view of worker-management relations as inherently punitive

jokes.text: How hard can it be? Hey, Dad, here's one. $28 an hour, plenty of fresh air... and you get to meet lots of interesting people. Ooh, what job is that? Grave digger.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing an appealing job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's grave digging with darkly comic job description
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with dark humor about making grave digging sound appealing

jokes.text: I wouldn't bury my turtle in that mud puddle. What's the use? Sheesh! What a slave driver.
jokes.setup: Homer being criticized for poor grave digging
jokes.punchline: Homer calling supervisor slave driver while being lazy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - blaming others for his own inadequacy

jokes.text: No, I didn't. Who is it, Frankenstein? The Boogerman?
jokes.setup: Strange sound from grave
jokes.punchline: Burns' childish monster references
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization - mixing sophistication with childish fears

jokes.text: The man in the bag. I think he's alive. Oh. Bad corpse. Stop scaring Smithers. Satisfied?
jokes.setup: Homer making noise in the body bag
jokes.punchline: Burns scolding Homer like a misbehaving pet
jokes.explanation: Absurd character moment treating a living person like a disobedient corpse

jokes.text: Damn it, Smithers! This isn't rocket science, it's brain surgery!
jokes.setup: Burns asking for an ice cream scoop for surgery
jokes.punchline: Inverting the common expression about rocket science being the hardest thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly inverts a common expression while maintaining Burns' character and the absurd surgical situation

jokes.text: Look at me-- I'm Davy Crockett!
jokes.setup: Burns performing brain surgery
jokes.punchline: Wearing Homer's scalp as a coonskin cap
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag that shows Burns' childish nature even in horrific circumstances

jokes.text: It's moving! It's alive! Oh, that fellow at Radio Shack said I was mad. Well, who's mad now?
jokes.setup: Robot coming to life
jokes.punchline: Classic Frankenstein reference with modern Radio Shack twist
jokes.explanation: Good Frankenstein parody updated with mundane modern reference

jokes.text: Hi, there. I'm your daddy.
jokes.setup: Robot Homer awakening
jokes.punchline: Burns' creepy paternal claim over his creation
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy moment that fits the Frankenstein pastiche

jokes.text: Mmm! Sprin-kles.
jokes.setup: Robot Homer's first words
jokes.punchline: Maintaining Homer's food obsession even as a robot
jokes.explanation: Perfect character continuity showing Homer's essence surviving mechanical transformation

jokes.text: And as for you, you clinking, clattering cacophony... of collagenous cogs and camshafts, take... that.!
jokes.setup: Burns attacking his robot creation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate alliterative insult before violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns vocabulary showcase with impressive alliteration and pompous delivery

jokes.text: Every bone... shattered... organs... leaking vital fluids... a slight headache, loss of appetite.
jokes.setup: Burns being severely injured by the robot
jokes.punchline: Understating massive trauma alongside minor symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from horrific injuries to mundane complaints, capturing Burns' oddly detached nature

jokes.text: You were crushing me. I tried to scream, but my mouth was full of flab.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he bit Homer
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of being smothered by Homer's body
jokes.explanation: Gross but effective physical comedy that visualizes the cramped sleeping situation

jokes.text: Perhaps you're wondering why you have two heads. My body was crushed, so I had head grafted onto your... shall we say, ample frame.
jokes.setup: Homer waking up confused
jokes.punchline: Burns explaining their grotesque fusion with typical politeness
jokes.explanation: Good horror-comedy moment maintaining Burns' polite euphemisms even in grotesque circumstances

jokes.text: Oh, I hate having two heads.
jokes.setup: Homer dealing with having Burns' head attached
jokes.punchline: Understated complaint about extraordinary situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer understatement treating the bizarre as merely annoying

jokes.text: Oh, that's right. It's all a dream... or is it?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince himself it's a dream
jokes.punchline: Burns creating uncertainty about reality
jokes.explanation: Standard dream uncertainty trope

tmdb_overview: Maggie uses one of three wishes; a mind-reading Bart rules Springfield; Homer donates his brain to Mr. Burns' science project.
insight: Halloween anthology hits 66 jokes in 22 minutes—Simpsons' densest stretch yet.
